cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about what advice Wesla Whitfield would most likely give to other disabled people based on the article. First, I need to look at what the article says about her. She was paralyzed from the waist down after a shooting when she was 29. She went through depression and even tried to kill herself. But then she recovered and learned to adjust. She mentions that the only thing she lost was the ability to walk, but she still had her mind, which is the most valuable asset. She also says that the only real disability is losing your mind. She also mentions that when she feels frustrated (like when everyone is dancing), she removes herself to focus on what she can do.
